Notebook Description¶

This notebook deep-dives into the analysis of forecasting bookings of an Entity, using a combination internal variables and macro-economic factors

The analysis are divided into multiple steps sequencially.

  1. Set up and Data Load
  2. Looking into the problem in hand - Target variables
  3. Data-preprocessing: Involving feature reduction and null value imputations
  4. Data Preparation: Involving segemnt-wise data split, feature creation & train-test split
  5. Feature Selection: Includes feature elimination, Algo selection and followed by feature selection
  6. Insights from Top features
  7. Using the Model to forecast and evaluate accuracies across different segment
  8. Model accuracy on Entity level forecast
  9. Future forecast
Notebook Setup and Data Load
The problem in hand - Target variables (EDA)
Key Takeaways
  1. Segment B mainly drives Entity Predictions
  2. Seasonality and trend observed in all time-series
  3. Proceeding with bottom up approach
Data Preprocessing

Looking into Nulls

Null Value Treatment (followed sequentially)

  1. Drop variable with almost no variance
  2. Use a single lead time by merging actual, predicted and requested lead time. Using a coalesce(actual,predicted,requested)
  3. forward-fill in cases of - quaterly reported variables and discontinued
  4. Fill 0 for temporary and one-off events
  5. Linear Interpolation of data at the end and middle of Time-series
Dropped columns :  ['Margin for 5/1-Year Adjustable Rate Mortgage in the United States (DISCONTINUED)', 'Origination Fees and Discount Points for 5/1-Year Adjustable Rate Mortgage in the United States (DISCONTINUED)']
lead_time_Segment_A created; non-null count: 125
lead_time_Segment_B created; non-null count: 117
lead_time_Segment_C created; non-null count: 120

Found 6 columns containing 'temporary':
 - Repurchase Agreements: Mortgage-Backed Securities Purchased by the Federal Reserve in the Temporary Open Market Operations
 - Overnight Repurchase Agreements: Federal Agency Securities Purchased by the Federal Reserve in the Temporary Open Market Operations
 - Overnight Repurchase Agreements: Mortgage-Backed Securities Purchased by the Federal Reserve in the Temporary Open Market Operations
 - Term Repurchase Agreements: Total Securities Purchased by the Federal Reserve in the Temporary Open Market Operations
 - Overnight Reverse Repurchase Agreements: Total Securities Sold by the Federal Reserve in the Temporary Open Market Operations
 - Reverse Repurchase Agreements: Total Securities Sold by the Federal Reserve in the Temporary Open Market Operations

Successfully filled missing values with 0 for the identified columns.

Identified 16 columns with a quarterly reporting pattern.
Successfully applied forward-fill to the quarterly columns.
Number of columns remaining after dropping >50% nulls: 159

 - shipping_component_cost_usd | Segment_B: 9 nulls remaining
 - shipping_component_cost_usd | Segment_C: 8 nulls remaining
 - shipping_component_net_price_usd | Segment_A: 7 nulls remaining
 - shipping_component_net_price_usd | Segment_B: 9 nulls remaining
 - shipping_component_net_price_usd | Segment_C: 8 nulls remaining
 - Net Percentage of Domestic Banks Tightening Standards for Subprime Mortgage Loans: 3 nulls remaining
 - S&P 500: 7 nulls remaining
 - Producer Price Index by Commodity: Telecommunication, Cable, and Internet User Services: Wireless Telecommunication Services: 15 nulls remaining
 - New Export Orders_Telecommunication Services: 10 nulls remaining

 - lead_time_Segment_B: 9 nulls remaining
 - lead_time_Segment_C: 8 nulls remaining

Nulls after treatment

Key takeaway
  • Null values at the beginning of the time-series are kept as Nulls.
Data Preparation

Creating separate datasets for Segments

Total Columns in original df: 159
------------------------------
Segment A DataFrame: 150 columns
Segment B DataFrame: 150 columns
Segment C DataFrame: 150 columns

Macroeconomic columns shared across segments: 146

Train-Test Split (70-30) sequentially

--- Train/Test Split Verification ---
Segment A | Train: 89 rows (70%) | Test: 39 rows (30%)
Segment B | Train: 89 rows (70%) | Test: 39 rows (30%)
Segment C | Train: 89 rows (70%) | Test: 39 rows (30%)

--- Split Boundaries ---
Segment A Train Ends: 2021-02-28 | Test Begins: 2021-03-31

Feature Creation followed by feature elimaination.

  1. Creating Lag variables of last 3 months for all variables including target
  2. Dropping any one of the indepent variables if they are very highly correlated - Abs Corr threshold set to 0.8
In segment A, number of features dropped from 600 to 125 due to high correlation 
In segment B, number of features dropped from 600 to 125 due to high correlation 
In segment C, number of features dropped from 600 to 125 due to high correlation 

Feature Selection

Feature selection is done using boosting model. The reason for choosing such a model was: 1) it handle non-linear relationships if its exists 2) It handles null values natively since, the training set has nulls

Insights from top feature other than lagged value of the target
Key Takeaways
  • Shipping component cost and lead time are key predictors for segment A
  • Macro-economic factors influences Segments B & C movements
Forecast for each segment and model evaluation

MAPE - key metric for business communication

Entity Level Prediction and its Accuracy
Future Forecasts

The future forecast is done by considering the lag values from all variables along-with forward-filling the macro-factors

=== NEXT 3 PERIODS FORECAST ===
Time Step | Segment A | Segment B | Segment C | OVERALL ENTITY
-----------------------------------------------------------------
  T + 1   |      0.27 |      1.63 |      0.46 |           2.36
  T + 2   |      0.27 |      1.62 |      0.48 |           2.38
  T + 3   |      0.28 |      1.62 |      0.50 |           2.40
